ety: pass. part. of ملّ 'to be wearied,' &c.; see malāl

(part. pass. of ملل Heb. and Aram. ) adj. Sad, dejected, grieved, melancholy, affected, fatigued, languid.

def: adj. corr. of معلول maʼlūl, q.v.
part. adj. Reprehended, reproached, blamed, accused.

def: part. adj. corr. of معلوم maʼlūm, q.v.
part. adj. Known; distinguished; celebrated, famous, notorious; clear, evident, certain, obvious, apparent; (in Geom.) given (as a line, &c.); — s.m. The active voice (of a verb)
more: adj. (ظاﮩر 1). 1. ( mālūma ) Known. 2. Apparent; obvious. Āp kī ilmīyat mālūm! ( Ironic .) mālūm k. , v. a. 1. To find out ( daryāft k . 1). 2. To recognize ( jānnā ). 3. To perceive; feel. 4. To understand; know. mālūm nahīṅ , I dont know. mālūm hotā hai , It seems; it appears; it is found. mālūm h. , v. n. 1. To be discovered, known. 2. To appear; seem. 3. To know by experience. Mālūm hogā hashr ko pīnā sharāb kā! Prov. You will know on the day of judgment what it is to drink wine. zurūrī mālūm h. , v. n. To be deemed necessary. maslahat mālūm hotā hai , It seems expedient. munāsib mālūm h . To seem proper.

(part. pass. of علم Heb. and Aram. ) adj. Known, distinguished, notorious, evident, apparent, obvious; (subst.) The active voice of a verb. معلوم کرنا maﻌlūm karnā, To find out, discover, understand, know, deem, suppose. معلوم هونا maﻌlūm honā, To appear, seem.
subs: (-ko), To know, understand; to discover, find out; to perceive, to behold; to recognize; to feel; to deem, suppose
It is not known to me; I do not know
It appears, it seems; it is found (to be)
(-ko, or -par), To be or become known (to), to be discovered; to be deemed, or supposed; to appear, seem.
ex: معلوم نہیں
mālūm nahī̃
(I) don't know
wety: From Arabic مَعْلُوم (maʕlūm) (through Classical Persian معلوم (ma'lūm)), from عَلِمَ (ʕalima).

s. plur. (of معلومة) Things known, sciences.

wety: Inherited from Sanskrit माम (māma), from Proto-Dravidian *māma.

def: s.f. Mother; — a respectful compellation for an old woman; — a woman who acts as a kind of general servant; a maid-servant
more: Mah. n. f. A maidservant. māmā-garī . n. f. The work of a maid-servant.

Sant. n. m. S. माम or मातुल Pr. māuo . 1. Maternal uncle. 1. Sāt māmā kā bhānjā nautā hī nautā ( yā bhūkā hī bhūkā ) ḍole . Prov. 1. The nephew of seven uncles he in feasts rejoiceth constantly. Or, 2. Who uncles seven doth possess, between them hungry goes I guess. 2. Apnā māmā mar mar gailan, julhā, dhuniyāṅ māmā bhailan . Bhoj. Prov. Whose uncles are all dead and gone weavers and carders for uncles own. 2. (Mah. Wom.) A snake. 3. (Hin. Wom.) A rat. māmī, māīṅ , Hin.; mumānī , Mah. n. f. S. मातुलानी Pālī, mātulānī , Maternal uncle's wife.
s. f. Mother. (Old women in general are so called).
s. m. A maternal uncle. See مامو māmū.
subs: s.f. The work of a maid-servant.

more: Wom. n. f. S. ममता attachment; Pr. vammaho . Affection, esp. maternal affection. Mātā bargī māmtā, saukan bargā bair, Dūjā ko rākhe nahīṅ, dekhā sāṅjh saver . Rus. A mother's love, a co-wife's hate, I ween From morn to eve its like was never seen.

def: s.f. Egoism; the sense of 'meum'; the interest, or affection, entertained for objects from the considering them as belonging to or connected with one's self; sense of ownership, or of self-interest; appropriation; selfishness; affection, attachment (to); — individuality; — self-sufficiency; pride, arrogance; covetousness, avarice
more: s. f. The interest or affection entertained for other objects from considering them as belonging to, or connected with oneʼs self, affection.
subs: vulg. mamtā-jukt, adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Filled with selfishness, self-interested, selfish; miserly; — an egotist, a selfish man, a miser.

def: s.m. = mamatā, q.v. s.f. Affection; maternal affection, &c. (see mamatā).

more: n. m. 1. A fountain; jet d'eau . 2. A water-cut from a canal to a field.
(noun of place of نبع Heb. and Aram. ) s. m. A fountain, jet dʼeau.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian منبع (“spring”).

direct plural; oblique/vocative singular

adj. Relating to the maternal uncle; as, مميرا بهاٴي mamerā bʼhāʼī, Motherʼs brotherʼs son, cousin. مميري بہن mamerī bahin, Motherʼs brotherʼs daughter.

more: n m. See چال 4, 6 & دستور A custom. māmūl ke din , A recurring period (menses). Har mahīne meṅ kuṛhāte the mujhe phūl ke ḍin. Bāre abke to mere ṭal gaë māmūl ke din . Wom. Raṅgīṅ.
(part. pass. of عمل Heb. and Aram. ) adj. 1. Made, prepared; (in gram.) governed. 2. Established; s. m. Custom, practice, habit, fixed allowance or gratuity.
subs: A recurring period (for a thing or act); the usual period (of menstruation).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَعْمُول (ma'mūl), borrowed from Arabic مَعْمُول (maʕmūl), from عَمِلَ (ʕamila).

def: adj. Customary, practised, usual, ordinary; current (as expenditure); due (time).
more: adj. Wonted; common ( chaltā , 5). māmūlī kharch , n. m. Current expenses. māmūlī savālāt , n. m. The ordinary questions. māmūlī kār ravāī , n. f. Ordinary practice or use. aiyām-i-māmūlī , n. m. Due time. dastūr-i-māmūlī , n. m. The ordinary course.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَعْمُولِی (ma'mūlī), from معمول + ـی (-ē). By surface analysis, مَعْمُول (ma'mūl, “usual”) + ی (ī).

def: s.m. = māmū = māmā, qq.v. s.m. A maternal uncle, &c. ( = māmā, q.v.). s.f. Mother; — a respectful compellation for an old woman; — a woman who acts as a kind of general servant; a maid-servant

Inhabited; peopled; well stocked with people, populous; colonized, cultivated, well-cultivated; in a flourishing state, flourishing; affluent, prosperous, happy; delightful; — in sound or good or efficient condition; in a state of good repair; — well-appointed (an army); — full; abundant, ample.
more: adj. عمر 1. Full; populous. populous. 2. Cultivated.
(part. pass. of عمر Heb. and Aram. ) adj. 1. Inhabited, frequented, populous; full, replete, complete. 2. Cultivated, happy, delightful, closed.

def: s.f. Population; populousness; cultivation; a flourishing condition; happiness; — fulness (as of a treasury, &c.); efficient condition, efficiency (of an army); — a state of good repair.

def: part. adj. Mixed; tempered.

def: s.f. Mother ( = mā, q.v.). s.m. (in Prosody) A molossus or metrical foot of three long syllables; — a magical or mystical formula; a name of Brahmā, of Vishṇu, and of Śiva.
more: n. m. S. मान 1. Respect (آرزو & ابهمان). 2. Arrogance. Mān kā bīṛā hīrā samān . Prov. Even betel offered with respect is as valuable as a diamond. 3. Measure of weight, length, or capacity. mān ḍhainā , v. n. To fall (one's pride). mān rakhnā , v. a. 1. To respect; honor. Huqqā Hari kā lāḍlā, sab kā rākhe mān, Bharī sabhā meṅ yūṅ phire jūṅ gopin meṅ Kān . The huqqa's Hari's darling, he respect shows every one, In full assembly he goes round, as mong the milkmaids Kān. 2. See kahnā k. mān rahnā, yā h. , Hin. Wom. v. n. To be dependant on. Merā sir kā sirdār jītā to maiṅ kiske mān rahtī? mān k. , v. a. To be proud. Yoh dhagṛā terā saë aṅt dhoī, Rahā kapaṭ sūṅ bhar, saukan, Dhagṛe par mān na kar saukan! Rus. This spouse of yours a rogue complete, Co-wife! full is he of deceit, Dont pride yourself on such a cheat! mān gumān , n. m. Pride ( abhimān ). mān marnā , v. a. To be humbled, prostrat…

s. m. 1. Character, dignity, honour, respect. 2. Haughtiness, arrogance, pride. 3. Blandishment. 4. Measure in general, whether of weight, length or capacity. 5. adj. Like. مان گمان mān-gumān, s. m. Dignity, state, honour. مان گون mān-gaun, Respect.
wety: From Sanskrit मान (māna).

def: s.m. The mind (considered as the seat of perception and passion); power of mind; intellect, intelligence, understanding, perception, sense; — the heart; soul; spirit; — inclination; will; purpose; — character, disposition, mood, temper
more: n. m. S. मन् to think. 1. Mind; spirit. 1. Man hamrā pās, dhan ankā pās . Bhoj. Prov. My mind is mine, his wealth is his. 2. Man jāne pāp, māī jāne na bāp . Prov. The heart its own sin knows; nor mother, father knows. 3. Jaisā man, vaisā dān . Prov. The gift as the donor. 4. Apne man se jāniye parāë man kī bāt . Prov. In your own mind another's purpose find. 5. Sabaṟ kar man meṅ, ta sukh lahe tan meṅ . Bhoj. Prov. Contented be your mind, your body ease shall find. 6. Man ke hāre hār hai, man ke jīte jīt . Prov. If you lose heart you lose, the brave heart wins. 7. Manvā mar gayā, khel bigaṛ gayā . Prov. Lose heart and you lose the game. 8. Name of a sacred lake and place of pilgrimage on Mount Kailās in the Himālaya mountains (the native place of the wild geese or swans; constant allusions are made to this lake in Hindī poetry, and the haṅs or rāj-haṅs (a kind of wild goose) is described as migrating to its shores every year at the breeding season)
more: n. m. S. मानुष or मनुष्य Pālī, manuso . A human being; a man. Mānas ke se hāth pāoṅ, mānas kī sī kāyā, Chār mahīne barkhā bītī, chhappar kyoṅ nahīṅ chhāyā ? A monkey. His hands and feet like man's, his frame for man's a match, It rained four months on end, why reared he not a thatch?
subs: s.m. An epithet of the river Sarjū (of which lake Mānas is the source).

ety: changeable in comp., before certain consonants, to mano
def: s.m. Mind (in its widest sense, as applied to all the mental powers), intellect, intelligence, understanding, perception, sense, conscience, will; (in Phil.) the internal organ of perception and cognition, the seat of perception and of passion (and reckoned by the followers of the Sānkhya and Nyāya systems as an organ of both sense and action); — thought, imagination, conception, idea, fancy; heart, affection, desire, longing; disposition, mood, temper, good-will, favour; spirit, energy, mettle (see man)
more: s. m. (dakh.) Man (in oppos. to woman: Harris ). See منکهہ manukh.
subs: s.m. Perfect consciousness; self-consciousness, attention of the mind to its own sensations
s.m. lit. 'Burning of the mind,' distress of mind, mental pain, anguish, agony; remorse, repentance, compunction, regret
s.f. Inclination or bias of the mind; heart's desire; wish, purpose, design

vulg. mano-gat, part. adj. Gone to the heart or mind; existing or sealed in the mind, resting or concealed in the mind or heart; passing in the mind; affecting the mind or soul; — desired; — s.m. What rests, or what is passing, in the mind; idea, thought, notion; wish, longing
part. adj. Cherished or concealed in the mind; thought or meditated on in secret
or mano-jāt, adj. & s.m. Sprung up in the mind or soul; mind-born, heart-born; — love; the god of love (i.q. Kām-dev)
vulg. mano-gya, adj. (f. -ā), Agreeable to the mind; heart-winning; pleasing, lovely, beautiful, charming
adj. (f. -ā), Taken by the mind, made choice of, wished for, approved, preferred; acceptable
and mano-bhūt, adj. & s.m. = mano-ja, q.v.

adj. (f. -ī), Consisting of spirit or mind; spiritual; mental; — (for mano-may-koś), the mental sheath (the second of the subtile sheaths in which the soul is cased, according to the Vedānta philosophy)
s.m. Defilement of the mind; impurity; sin
s.m. Application of the mind, attention
s.m. The act of pleasing the mind; pleasantness, &c. (i.q. man-ranjan, q.v.s.v. man)
corr. manorth, s.m. (lit. 'vehicle of the mind'), Wish, desire; design, intention, object, purpose, aim; — fancy, imagination; good pleasure; desired object
or manorath su-phal honā, v.n. A desire to be accomplished or fulfilled; an object to be gained
v.n. To form a desire, or a design, &c.; to purpose, &c.
adj. (f. -ā), Delighting or gratifying the mind; satisfactory; attractive, pleasing, interesting, agreeable, charming, lovely, beautiful; — s.m. A charming or lovely person, &c.
s.m. Eagerness of mind, fancy, caprice, whim, freak
adj. Full, complete; developed (said of the mind)
s.f. State or frame of mind, disposition, temper; — operation, or activity, of the mind; volition, wish, desire, fancy

adj. Heart-stealing, heart-ravishing, captivating the heart or mind, taking the fancy, fascinating, bewitching, charming, pretty, attractive, interesting, delightful, pleasing, beautiful, lovely; — s.m. (f. -ī), A charming person, &c.
vulg. mano-hartā, or mano-hartāʼī, s.f., and mano-haratva, s.m. Captivatingness, charmingness, attractiveness, pleasantness, fascination, beauty
s.f. A charming, fascinating, or lovely woman

def: s.f. Mind, thought; wish, desire, inclination; intention purport, design, purpose; — name of a goddess of the serpent race, the particular protectress of mankind from the venom of serpents
more: n. f. S. मानस Cont. of H. man mind, āsā hope. A. manshā . Wish; desire ( ās , 1, 2). mansā pāp , Evil thinking; sinning in thought. mansā pūran k. , v. a. To fulfil one's hopes. mansā-devī , n. f. The goddess of the serpent race, and refuge against its venom.
s. f. Wish, desire, intention, design, purport, purpose.
subs: s.m. Wickedness or sin of thought or desire; evil-thinking
(apnī), To fulfil or to gratify a wish, &c.
s.m. A proper name; — a name jocularly applied to a monkey

def: s.f. = S مانستو मानसत्व mānasatva, s.m. Thoughtfulness; spirituality.
s.m. = S مانستا मानसता mānasatā, s.f. Thoughtfulness; spirituality.

ety: S. मनुष्य+ भेद+कः; but cf. next
def: s.m. (dialec. or rustic) A man, a male.

ety: S. मनुष्य+ भेद+कः; but cf. next
def: s.m. (dialec. or rustic) A man, a male.

ety: S. मनसि, loc. of manas, q.v.+जः
def: adj. Born or generated in the mind; mind-born; heart-born; mental, intellectual; — s.m. Love; — the god of love (i.q. Kām-dev; cf. mano-ja, s.v. manas.
more: s. m. Cupid. See کام ديو kām-dew.

def: adj. Of or pertaining to the mind (manas); mental; spiritual; rational; ideal, imaginary.
adj. Of or relating to the mind, mental, intellectual.

s.m. See s.v. manas. adj. Belonging to the mind or spirit, mental, spiritual; — s.m. Mind, the seat or faculty of reason and feeling; heart, soul; spirit; thought, wish, volition; — mānas, or mānas-sarowar, vulg. mānsarowar, mānsarwar, s.m. Name of a sacred lake and place of pilgrimage on Mount Kailās in the Himālaya mountains (the native place of the wild geese or swans; constant allusions are made to this lake in Hindī poetry, and the haṅs or rāj-haṅs (a kind of wild goose) is described as migrating to its shores every year at the breeding season)
subs: s.m. An epithet of the river Sarjū (of which lake Mānas is the source).

ety: for A. منصة manassat, &c., n. of place fr. نصّ 'to display or exhibit'
def: s.m. A place where anything is displayed or exhibited, a theatre; — the marriage chamber or bed (richly ornamented); — a raised seat (highly decorated) on which a bride is exhibited to public view.
more: (noun of place, &c. from نصّ) s. m. The tent or chamber of the bride.

def: s.m. See s.v. manas. adj. Belonging to the mind or spirit, mental, spiritual; — s.m. Mind, the seat or faculty of reason and feeling; heart, soul; spirit; thought, wish, volition; — mānas, or mānas-sarowar, vulg. mānsarowar, mānsarwar, s.m. Name of a sacred lake and place of pilgrimage on Mount Kailās in the Himālaya mountains (the native place of the wild geese or swans; constant allusions are made to this lake in Hindī poetry, and the haṅs or rāj-haṅs (a kind of wild goose) is described as migrating to its shores every year at the breeding season)
subs: s.m. An epithet of the river Sarjū (of which lake Mānas is the source).

def: adj. Intelligent; intellectual; spiritual.
s.f. Likeness, resemblance.
more: s. f. (dakh.) 1. Purpose, vow: see منت mannat or مانتا māntā. 2. Likeness.

def: (for A. مناة), s.m.(?) One of the three chief idols of the citizens of Mecca in times of paganism (the others being allāt, and ʻuzzā).
more: An idol of the Arabs before they became Muhammadans.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَنَات (manāt), from Arabic مَنَاة (manāh).

ety: S. मान्यता, or मानता, or mān(nā) + at = Prk. अत्ती = S. आप्तिः (i.e. आपि caus. aug.+aff. ति
def: s.f. Obedience; belief, religious belief; — voluntary obligation, vow; promise; resolution, purpose; — acknowledgment, assent, acceptance, acquiescence (syn. mannat.)
s.f. Pride, arrogance; — honour, respect, dignity, &c. ( = mān, q.v.).

ety: manā(nā)+Prk. आवत्तिआ = S. आप्तिका (i.e. आपि caus. aug.+aff. ति+का
def: s.f. Satisfaction, assurance; security, surety, bail; a person referred to for payment of the debt, &c. of another; — an acknowledgment; an acceptance; — (dialec.) a premium or bonus to a money-lender above the stipulated interest
more: n. f. S. मानित. 1. Satisfaction ( khātir-jamā ). 2. Surety (ضاصني). 3. An acceptance. manautī-dār , n. m. One who becomes a surety for a consideration, esp. one who is a security to government for a revenue payment.
s. f. Bail, security, a person referred to to pay the debt, &c. of another, surety, assurance, acknowledgment.
subs: s.m. One who becomes surety for another; one who is a security to government for a revenue-payment.

def: s.m. A youth, lad, youngster, juvenile; a little man, manikin (used contemptuously); — a pearl necklace of sixteen strings.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit मानव (mānava).

def: adj. Descended from man, or from Manu; belonging or proper to man; human; — s.m. A human being, a man; — a lad, boy, &c. ( = māṇav, q.v.); — mankind; — a multitude of men.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit मानव (mānava).

def: s.m. A lad or boy not exceeding sixteen years of age; a juvenile; — a little man, a dwarf, a manikin; — a childish or ignorant man.

adj. (f. -vatī), See s.v. māna or mān. s.m. Good opinion of self (or of any person or thing, &c. connected with self), conceit; self-confidence; pride, haughtiness, arrogance; — honour, respect, reverence, consideration, regard for others; paying honour (to), honourable reception or estimation; — dignity, rank, character, reputation; worth, value; — right, claim, title. privilege; — place, province, proper office or business; — a wounded sense of honour, anger or indignation (excited by jealousy, &c.); female caprice; sulkiness; blandishment; — an agent; — a blockhead; — a barbarbian
subs: (˚na+ap˚), s.m. Honour and dishonour; esteem and disesteem
s.m. Loss of honour, injury to reputation, dishonour, disgrace, indignity, humiliation
s.m. Honour, respect; — the rights of an official or person in authority; the privileges and perquisites attached to an office

(-kā), To show respect (to), treat with honour; to respect, reverence, honour; — to pay heed (to), to mind, to obey. — mān-ṛahit, adj. (f. -ā), Without honour, unhonoured, despised; — wanting in respect, disrespectful
or mān-sanwān, s.m. Honourable reception; welcome
(-ke), To be dead to pride (for), to lose all pride, to be humbled or crushed (by calamity)
(-kā), To be vain or proud (of); to give oneself airs
s.m. Honour, dignity, state; — pride, haughtiness
or mān-vant, or mān-vān, adj. (f. -matī), Possessing honour or pride; proud, haughty, disdainful; high-spirited; angry, indignant
s.f. A proud woman, a haughty or disdainful woman
adj. (f. -ā), Stripped of honour; destitute of pride, humble, lowly
s.f. Loss of honour or respectability
adj. (f. -ā), Void of haughtiness or pride; destitute of honour or respectability
adj. (f. -ā), Connected or associated with honour; possessing honour or reputation.

def: s.f. (of mānava), A daughter of man or Manu, a woman.
s.f. The wife of a Manu.

adj. Of or relating to meaning or intended sense; flowing from the sense (opp. to lafz̤ī, 'verbal'); significant; expressive (of); — of or relating to idea, or mind, or intellect; ideal, mental, intellectual (opp. to ḥissī)
more: adj. معني meaning. 1. Intrinsic; essential. 2. Understood; implied.
(from معني) adj. 1. Essential, intrinsick, real. 2. Significant.
subs: real; intrinsic; essential; absolute; understood, implied.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَعْنَوِی (ma'nawī), from Arabic مَعْنَوِيّ (maʕnawiyy).

def: v.t. = manānā, q.v. v.t. To cause to mind; to put in mind (of, -se); to cause to agree to, &c.; to cause to trust; to reason with, to persuade, prevail upon, win over; to soothe; to coax; to conciliate; to propitiate; to assuage, to appease; — to turn the mind or attention to; to pay (one's) devoirs to; to invoke, to call upon (God); to desire (of, -se), to pray for; — to meditate, reflect upon; — to practise, to carry on (?)
subs: v.t. To conciliate, to appease, &c. (i.q. manānā).

def: v.t. = manānā, q.v. v.t. To cause to mind; to put in mind (of, -se); to cause to agree to, &c.; to cause to trust; to reason with, to persuade, prevail upon, win over; to soothe; to coax; to conciliate; to propitiate; to assuage, to appease; — to turn the mind or attention to; to pay (one's) devoirs to; to invoke, to call upon (God); to desire (of, -se), to pray for; — to meditate, reflect upon; — to practise, to carry on (?)
subs: v.t. To conciliate, to appease, &c. (i.q. manānā).

def: s.f. The wife of Manu (i.q. manāvī).
s.f. pl. of manzil, q.v.
more: s. plur. of منزل manzil, q. v.

def: scenes; views
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَنَاظِر (manāzir), from Arabic مَنَاظِر (manāẓir).

def: s.f. (dialec.) Thought, word, and deed.
s.m. = māćā, q.v. s.m. A platform, a frame or stage (on which a watchman sits to drive away birds, &c. from corn-fields); — a large bedstead, a bed
subs: s.m. 'Bed-breaker'; one who sticks to his bed, a lazy person who rarely stirs from his bed; — a kind of soldier among the Rājpūts (very indolent and much addicted to opium, but active and brave when roused)
adj. & s.m. Stupid, foolish; — a stupid or foolish fellow.

def: s.m. A platform, stage, scaffold, &c. (i.q. manć, q.v.).

s.m. A stage, or platform, a scaffold: an elevated shed raised on bamboos in a field (where a watchman is stationed to protect the crop from cattle, birds, &c., — syn. maćān); — a bedstead; a chair; a sort of throne, or chair of state, or the platform on which it is raised, the dais; a high place; an alcove; an altar.
more: s. m. A platform, a scaffold; a sort of throne or chair of state, or the platform on which it is raised, the dais; a bed, a bedstead.

def: adj. See s.v. man. s.f. Mother ( = mā, q.v.). s.m. (in Prosody) A molossus or metrical foot of three long syllables; — a magical or mystical formula; a name of Brahmā, of Vishṇu, and of Śiva.
more: adj. Assiduous, intent, eager, active, bold.

def: s.m. (dialec.) = maćān, q.v. s.m. A platform; scaffold; stage; raised seat; bench; a large bedstead.

s.f. = māćī, q.v. s.f. A four-legged stool; — a small bedstead; — a yoke for oxen (formed of two pieces of wood); — a harrow (a square piece of wood on which a man sits); — a bag of net-work behind a cart; the rumble, the boot.
Manchester (a city in England)
ex: چناچه سنه ۱۷۷۰ عیسوئي کو مانچسٿر اور لیورپول شہر کے درمیان ایک نہر کھودنے کي
cunācih sanh 1770 ʻīsva'ī ko māncisṭar aur līvarpūl śahar ke darmīyān ek nahar khodne kī
Therefore in 1770 A.D. [a plan] to dig a canal between the cities of Manchester and Liverpool
اس کے بعد بٹ مجھے راچڈیل کی طرف لے کر روانہ ہوا –– وہاں پہنچے تو اپنا دوست مانچسٹر آیا ہوا تھا۔
Māḍarn Ibn-i Bat̤ūt̤ah
After that, Butt took me towards Rochdale; when we reached there, our friend had arrived in Manchester.
wety: Borrowed from English Manchester, influenced by languages such as Punjabi مانچَسْٹَر (māncasṭar) / مَنْچَسْٹَر (mancasṭar) or Pahari-Potwari مَنْچَسْٹَر (mancasṭar), as opposed to the direct borrowing of مینچسٹر (maincisṭar).

def: s.m. A platform, &c. ( = manć and manćā); a boat ( = maćwā).
adj. Faded; dull, faint; tarnished; eclipsed
more: adj. S. मन्द 1. Faded; tarnished 2. Eclipsed. māṅd , n. f. 1. (W.) A heap of cowdung; a midden; dunghill. Hoṅṭh moṭe haiṅ, ki uple māṅd ke, Yā ki haiṅ tūṭe kināre nāṅd ke? A satire. Are these thick lips I see, or cow-dung pats, Or edges broken off from earthen vats? 2. (E.) A hole ( bhaṭ , 2). māṅd paṛ jānā, yā ho jānā , v. n. 1. To be faded; to become dull. 2. To be outshone, eclipsed. The lahroṅ meṅ ek chāṅd ke lākh chāṅd, Jinheṅ dekh-kar dhūp hojāë māṅd . Sohan Lāl. The ripples made a thousand moons of one, Did the sun see eclipsed would be the sun.
n. m. 1. See پیچ Māṛ na jūṛe, tāṛī! Bhoj. Prov. He cannot command even congee, and he calls for toddy! 2. (S. मण्ड, H. māṅḍī ) Starch. 3. A class of songs peculiar to Mārwāṛ.
s. f. 1. Faded, a dull colour. 2. A dunghill. 3. The den of a wild beast.
subs: or māṅd ho-jānā, v.n. To fade; to become dull or dimmed; to become eclipsed.

def: s.f. A dull colour; — a heap of dung (esp. cow-dung); a dunghill; — den (of a wild beast), lair, haunt, hole (of a fox, &c.).
s.m. Rice-water, rice-gruel; starch; paste; — a class of songs peculiar to Mārwār.
more: s. m. Rice-water, rice-gruel, starch, paste.

ety: Pehl. mañṭ; Zend mañṭ; S. मन्त् (मत्); cf. H. mant
def: A suffix joined to substantives to form adjectives denoting 'possession,' &c. (e.g. daulat - mand, 'wealthy, rich'; — ʻaql-mand, 'sensible, wise').
more: An affix signifying full of, as aql-mand sensible; daulat-mand rich.
n. f. The raised rim of a well.

n. m. muṅḍiyā , n. f. S. मुण्ड, Pālī, munḍo . 1. The head. 2. ( muṅḍh, muḍh ) Chief; head; principal. munḍ-chirā , n. m. 1. A sect of mendicants who extort alms by threatening to wound themselves. 2. An extortioner. munḍ-chirāpan , n. m. Extortion. muṅḍkar'ī mārnā , v. n. To sleep with the head between or on the legs drawn up. Gaī muṅḍkarī mār ākhir ko leṭ, Chhapar-khaṭ ke kone meṅ sar muṅh lapeṭ . Mīr. Hasan. munḍ-māl , n. f. A necklace of human heads worn by Mahadeva.
1. A particle which, added to nouns, implies endowed with, or having; as, عقلمند aﻌḳl-mand, Sensible; دولتمند daulat-mand, Rich. 2. s. A sort of amber, which is black, stony, and heavy.
1. adj. Abated, slow, tedious, dull, foolish. 2. Bad, ill. 3. s. m. The planet Saturn. 4. Saturday. مندگت mand-gati, Slow (in) pace; slowly, softly. مند مند mand-mand, adv. Gently, softly.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ـمَند (-mand).

def: s.m.(?) A species of jet, or black amber (stony and heavy).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ـمَند (-mand).

def: s.m. Skimmings; scum, froth, foam; barm; rice-water, gruel; starch, paste; — the best part (of anything), essence, pith, cream; — the head; — ornament, decoration; — s.f. The raised rim (of a well).
adj. (f. -ī) = مانده mānda, q.v.
more: Hin. n. m. pl. māṅḍe . See chapātī .

ety: fr. māndan = Pehl. māntan; rt. Zend man = S. मन्
def: adj. (f. -ī), Left, remaining; — fatigued, tired, weary, languid; ailing, indisposed (Pers. pl. māndogān, 'the weary,' &c.)
more: adj. 1. ( In comp .) Remained; left. 2. ( thakā māṅdah ) Tired; fatigued; weary. 3. Ailing ( bīmār , 1). dar-māṅdah , adj. 1. Fatigued; tired. 2. Without means.
(from ماندن) adj. 1. Remained, left. 2. Tired, weary, fatigued, ailing, indisposed, languid. (Pers. plur. ماندگان).
subs: Fatigued, tired; broken down; without means; indigent.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَانْدَه (mānda), past participle of مَانْدَن (māndan, “to stay, remain”).

def: s.m. A sort of pastry, a thin cake (syn. ćapātī); — film, speck, web (on the pupil of the eye), a cataract.
more: s. m. 1. Film, speck (on the eye). 2. A kind of bread.

def: adj. (f. -ā, or -ī), Slow, tardy, inactive, sluggish; cold, phlegmatic, apathetic; tedious; dull, languid; stupid, heavy, doltish, foolish; dull (as a market, or as light, &c.), dim, faint (as light, or sound, &c.); low, deep, hollow, soft (sound, or voice); mild, gentle; affable; moderate; cheap; abated, allayed, lulled, slackened, relaxed; slack; weak; slight, trifling, little, small; — sick, ill; bad, vile, wicked; coarse
more: adj. S. मन्द Pālī, mando . 1. Abated; allayed; lulled (wind). Nikso chandā to andher bhayo mandā . Prov. When the moon appears the darkness flies. (Falsehood pales before truth). 2. Dull (market) ( sust , 7). 3. Moderate; low ( sastā ). 4. Low (tone). mandā, mandvāṛā , n. m. 1. Dullness of the market; depression of trade. 2. Decrease; diminution. Kām kā mandā hai. mandā bolnā , v. n. 1. To speak in a low tone. 2. To become dull (a market). mand-bkāgī , Unfortunate; unhappy. mandā bechnā, yā lagānā , v. a. To sell cheap. mandā paṛnā, yā h., mandānā v. n. 1. To be abated, lulled. 2. To become dull; to decline (a business). 3. To be appeased, pacified. mandā tārā , ( Ast .) Uranus. mandā k. , v. a. To abate; mitigate; remit. mand-mat , adj. Stupid; silly. mandī āṅch , A slow fire. mandī-budh , Mental imbecility. mandī dūkān , 1. A cheap shop. 2. A slow shop. mandī hai , (The trade) is depre…
adj. 1. Shaven. 2. Docked. 3. Not pointed (a shoe). munḍā , n. m. 1. (Panj.) A boy. 2. A disciple ( chelā ). 3. A boot. 4. The Hindi running hand in which the vowel marks are left out.
n. m. S. मण्ड 1. A thin cake ( phulkā ). 2. E. A sweetmeat. 3. Film; speck (on the eye).
adj. Gentle, mild, affable, cheap, abated, allayed, slow, tardy: little.
s. m. (dakh.) Flock (of cattle), swarm, bevy, herd, drove, class, covey, hatch (of chickens, &c. Harris ).
subs: s.m. The planet Saturn; — an epithet of Saturday; — an epithet of Yama; — adv. Slowly; softly, &c. (see mand; and mand-mand); — mandā, s.m. Slowness; languidness; slackness; decrease, diminution, abatement; dulness (of a market), depression (of trade); cheapness, &c.
(˚da+ āt˚), adj. Slow-minded, slow of apprehension; simple, foolish

(˚da+ād˚), adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Having little respect (for another), caring little (for), disrespectful; disregarding, neglecting, neglectful, inattentive, remiss, slack; — s.m. A disrespectful person, &c.
adj. Slow of apprehension, slow-witted, possessed of little understanding, dull, stupid, foolish, senseless; wanting in energy; indifferent; bad
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Unfortunate, ill-fated, unhappy, wretched; — an unfortunate person, unhappy wight, &c.
s.m., or mand-bhāgyatva, s.m., or mand-bhāgyatā, s.f. Adverse fortune, ill-fortune, ill-fatedness, misfortune
v.t. To retard; to slacken (speed, &c.); to abate, to diminish, &c.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Slow-moving, going or marching slowly; — walking in evil ways, given to evil courses; — one who moves or marches slowly, &c.
adj. Having a slow motion, moving slowly; — s.f. Slow motion; slow progress; — adv. Slowly, softly
adv. Slowly, tardily, sluggishly; gently, softly; slightly; badly
vulg. mand-bis, adj. (f. -ā), Containing but little poison, having but little venom; — s.m. A kind of snake
(˚da+ud˚), s.f. Name of Rāvaṇ's favourite wife (mother of Indra-jit)
s.m. A gentle laugh; a smile, simper
v.n. To speak in a low tone; — to become slack or dull (a market)
v.t. To sell at a low or moderate price, to sell cheap
v.n. To be or become abated, or slackened, or lulled, &c.; to fall (as a market); to become dull, to decline (a business); — to be allayed, or appeased, or pacified; to abate, &c.
v.t. To abate, slacken, relax; to mitigate; to remit
v.t. To put a low price on, to sell cheap
A slow shop; — a cheap shop

def: adj. & s.m. (P. also manda, s.) = mandā, q.v.
s.m. A kind of pastry, a thin cake (syn. phulkā); — a sort of sweetmeat (made into small balls or nuts); — a film, speck, web (on the pupil of the eye), a cataract (i.q. māṅḍā, q.v.).
more: s. m. Name of a sweetmeat. ¶ munḍā ( मुंड ) 1. s. m. The head, the head of a paper kite. 2. adj. Shaven, bald; uncovered. 3. A kind of uncovered pālkī.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀫𑀼𑀁𑀟 (muṃḍa) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-), from Sanskrit मुण्ड (muṇḍa).

def: adj. (f. -ā, or -ī), Slow, tardy, inactive, sluggish; cold, phlegmatic, apathetic; tedious; dull, languid; stupid, heavy, doltish, foolish; dull (as a market, or as light, &c.), dim, faint (as light, or sound, &c.); low, deep, hollow, soft (sound, or voice); mild, gentle; affable; moderate; cheap; abated, allayed, lulled, slackened, relaxed; slack; weak; slight, trifling, little, small; — sick, ill; bad, vile, wicked; coarse
subs: s.m. The planet Saturn; — an epithet of Saturday; — an epithet of Yama; — adv. Slowly; softly, &c. (see mand; and mand-mand); — mandā, s.m. Slowness; languidness; slackness; decrease, diminution, abatement; dulness (of a market), depression (of trade); cheapness, &c.
(˚da+ āt˚), adj. Slow-minded, slow of apprehension; simple, foolish

(˚da+ād˚), adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Having little respect (for another), caring little (for), disrespectful; disregarding, neglecting, neglectful, inattentive, remiss, slack; — s.m. A disrespectful person, &c.
adj. Slow of apprehension, slow-witted, possessed of little understanding, dull, stupid, foolish, senseless; wanting in energy; indifferent; bad
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Unfortunate, ill-fated, unhappy, wretched; — an unfortunate person, unhappy wight, &c.
s.m., or mand-bhāgyatva, s.m., or mand-bhāgyatā, s.f. Adverse fortune, ill-fortune, ill-fatedness, misfortune
v.t. To retard; to slacken (speed, &c.); to abate, to diminish, &c.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Slow-moving, going or marching slowly; — walking in evil ways, given to evil courses; — one who moves or marches slowly, &c.
adj. Having a slow motion, moving slowly; — s.f. Slow motion; slow progress; — adv. Slowly, softly
adv. Slowly, tardily, sluggishly; gently, softly; slightly; badly
vulg. mand-bis, adj. (f. -ā), Containing but little poison, having but little venom; — s.m. A kind of snake
(˚da+ud˚), s.f. Name of Rāvaṇ's favourite wife (mother of Indra-jit)
s.m. A gentle laugh; a smile, simper
v.n. To speak in a low tone; — to become slack or dull (a market)
v.t. To sell at a low or moderate price, to sell cheap
v.n. To be or become abated, or slackened, or lulled, &c.; to fall (as a market); to become dull, to decline (a business); — to be allayed, or appeased, or pacified; to abate, &c.
v.t. To abate, slacken, relax; to mitigate; to remit
v.t. To put a low price on, to sell cheap
A slow shop; — a cheap shop
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ـمَند (-mand).

def: adj. = S منداتما मन्दात्मा mandātmā, adj. See s.v. mand.
s. (dakh.) See منڐپ manḍap.
more: s. (dakh.) A place for the reception of travellers, a choultry or caravansary, &c.

def: s. (dakh.) See منڐپ manḍap.
s.f. Fatigue, weariness, lassitude; — illness, indisposition.
more: n. f. 1. Illness; indisposition Is māṅdgī ne hameṅ to khā līyā . Wom. 2. Fatigue ( thakān ).

def: s.f. The stream of the celestial Ganges; (poet.) the galaxy or milky way; — a species of Sanskrit metre; — (in Astron.) name of a particular conjunction.
s.f. = maṇdalī = maṇḍal, q.v. s.f. (dialec.) = mandalī, or manḍal, qq.v.
n. f. The iron-ring round the mouth of a charas , q. v.
s.f. A kind of drum (beaten with the fingers), a kettle-drum.
s.f. (dialec.) 1˚ = منڐل manḍal, q.v.; — 2˚ A fountain, jet dʼeau. s.f. = maṇdalī = maṇḍal, q.v. s.f. (dialec.) = mandalī, or manḍal, qq.v.
s.m. (dialec.), corr. of bandar, 'port, harbour,' q.v.

s.m. Disk of the sun or moon; a disc, circle, ring, coil (of a snake), band (of iron, &c.), orbit (of a planet, &c.); (in Astron.) a great circle; a halo (round the sun or moon); the sensible horizon; the celestial vault; the sky; a wheel; a circumference; a circular figure or diagram; a sort of mystical diagram (formed in summoning a divinity (upon occasions); compass, wheel, turn, whirl; — a globe, ball, sphere, orb; — a round tent or house; — a nest; — a circus, an arena, an amphitheatre; — a region; country, territory, district, province (extending twenty, or, according to some, forty yojanas in every direction); the country over which the twelve princes termed Ćakravartī are supposed to have reigned (whence the term maṇḍal at the end of certain modern names, e.g. Coromandel, i.e. Kuru-maṇḍal); — a company, an assembly, a band, an association (i.q. maṇḍalī, q.v.); — a sort of leprosy with circular spots; — (for S. मण्डलः) a circular array of troops, an army drawn up in a circle; — a kind of harmless snake; — headman (of a village); an officer (employed in a village), an exciseman; — a ringleader
more: s. m. 1. A circle, orb, sphere, disk (of the sun or moon), halo, circumference. 2. A round tent. 3. An officer employed in villages, exciseman.
subs: To form a circle, or a ring; to form oneself (or themselves, itself, &c.) into a circle or ring; — to spread (over), to overspread, to lower (as clouds)
(˚la+īś˚), s.m. Lord of a district, ruler or governor of a province
s.m. The zodiacal circle, the zodiac.

def: s.m. (dialec.) = mandar = mandir, q.v. s.m. The coral tree, Erythrina indica; one of the five trees in Indra's paradise; — swallow-wort, Asclepias gigantea, or a white variety of Calotropis gigantea.
more: n. f. S. मर्द्दल A kettledrum.

n. m. S. मण्डल a circle. 1. A circle; circumference; disk. 2. Sphere; orb. 3. A round tent or house. 4. The sky ( ākās , 1). 5. An arena; theatre; circus. 6. A region; country. Brij manḍal des basā rasyā! Wom. 7. The headman of a village. manḍal bāṅdhnā , v. n. To be overcast; to lower (clouds). manḍal desh, paṭkā , ( Ast .) A zone; a tropic. rās-manḍal ; S. क्रान्तिवृत; A. mintaqat-ul-burūj , n. m. The zodiac.
s. f. A kind of drum made of wood and beat with the fingers. ¶ (dakh.) See منڐل manḍal.
s. f. 1. A fountain, a jet dʼeau. 2. (Corrupt. of بندر) A port, harbour.

def: adj. See s.v. maṇḍal, s.f. = maṇdalī = maṇḍal, q.v. s.f. (dialec.) = mandalī, or manḍal, qq.v.

def: v.n. To make a circuit; to fly round, to hover (as birds); to be enveloped
more: v. n. To make a circuit, to hover (as birds, &c.), to be enveloped.
subs: (maṇḍlāyā, p.p. of maṇḍlānā), To go wandering round or about; to keep hovering round.

def: s.f. An epithet of the goddess Kālī; — name of the wife of king Alark.
s.m. (lit. 'marked with round spots; ringed'), A snake; — a cat.
s.f. (dialec.) = mandalī, or manḍal, qq.v.

s.f. A circle, a disk, a ring, a coil, &c. (i.q. maṇḍal, of wh. it is the dim. form); — a nest; — a company, an assembly, a congregation; a band, an association, a corporation; a flock; a church; a sect; — bent grass, Panicum dactylon
more: s. f. An assembly, a corporation.
subs: s.m. Lord or ruler of a district or province (i.q. maṇdeleśvar); head or chief of a company, or an association, &c.

def: s.m. Ruler or governor of a district or province (i.q. maṇḍaleśvar, q.v.s.v. maṇḍal).
s.m. A tumbler pigeon (syn. loṭan).
s.m. The act of ornamenting or adorning; bedecking oneself; — ornament, decoration, embellishment; jewels, trinkets, &c.
s. m. (dakh.) Flock (of cattle), swarm, bevy, herd, drove, class, covey, hatch (of chickens, &c. Harris ).
s. (dakh.) The world.

ety: mandā˚ = mand, q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. आव or आवे = S. आपि caus. augment
def: v.n. To become slow or languid, &c. (see mand); to become abated, &c. (i.q. mandā paṛnā, q.v.s.v. mandā).

s.m. An open hall; a temporary building or shed (adorned with flowers, and erected on festive occasions, such as marriages, &c.); a pavilion; a shed; — a building consecrated to a deity, a temple; — an arbour, a bower
more: s. m. 1. A temporary building or open shed adorned with flowers, a house. 2. A temple. منڐپ اطلسي manḍap-i-at̤las-ī (dakh.) The lower heaven or crystalline sphere.
subs: s.m. The lower heaven, or crystalline sphere.

def: n. m. 1. A temporary building or shed. 2. A temple.
s.m. The coral tree, Erythrina indica; one of the five trees in Indra's paradise; — swallow-wort, Asclepias gigantea, or a white variety of Calotropis gigantea.
more: s. m. 1. The name of a mountain, famous in India, with which the ocean was churned. 2. ( मंदार ) Name of a plant. (Asclepias gigantea). See آک āk.

def: adj. Slow, tardy, sluggish, &c. (i.q. mand, q.v.); large, bulky; thick, dense; firm; — s.m. Name of a sacred mountain (with which the ocean is said to have been churned by the gods and Asurs for the recovery of the amr̤it and thirteen other precious things lost during the Deluge); — a name of paradise or heaven (svarga); — name of a tree of paradise, or one of the five trees in Indra's heaven; — mandarāćal (˚ra+ać˚), s.m. The mountain Mandar
more: n. m. S. मन्दिर. 1. A house; dwelling-place; mansion. 1. Sukh mandar māṅ baiṭh chain sūṅ! Sukh ke rāg gavā, gori! Jāpe māṅ bār na jā, gorī! Rus. Wom. 2. Rahas rahas gale lago, piharvā? Āī hai gorī tere maṅdarva . Wom. 2. A temple; pagoda. 3. The body. Is mandar ke das darvāje . Hymn. rāj-mandar , n. m. A royal place.
subs: (˚ra+āl˚), s.f. An epithet of the goddess Kālī (i.q. mandālasā)
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit मन्दिर (mandira).

adj. (f. -ī), Short, stumpy, squat, dapper, thick-set.
